back_translation: When the Lord your God brings you to the land where you are going to possess it, and He drives out many nations from before you—the Hittite and the Girgashite and the Amorite and the Canaanite and the Perizzite and the Hivite and the Jebusite, seven nations greater and mightier than you—
and the Lord your God gives them before you and you strike them, devote them, you shall devote them. You shall not cut a covenant with them, and you shall not show them favor.
And you shall not intermarry with them. Your daughter you shall not give to his son, and his daughter you shall not take for your son.
For he will turn your son away from following Me, and they will serve other gods, and the anger of the Lord will burn against you, and He will destroy you quickly.

But this is what you shall do to them: their altars you shall tear down, and their sacred pillars you shall break, and their Asherah poles you shall cut down, and their carved images you shall burn in the fire.
For a holy people you are to the Lord your God. In you the Lord your God has chosen to be His people of treasured possession, out of all the peoples that are on the face of the ground.
Not because you were more numerous than all the peoples did the Lord set His affection on you and choose you, for you were the fewest of all the peoples.

But because of the Lord's love for you, and because of His keeping the oath that He swore to your fathers, the Lord brought you out with a strong hand, and He redeemed you from the house of slaves, from the hand of Pharaoh king of Egypt.

And you shall know that the Lord your God—He is God, the faithful God, who keeps the covenant and the loving devotion to those who love Him and to those who keep His commandments, for a thousand generations.
But repaying those who hate Him to their face, to destroy them; He will not delay—to the one who hates Him, to his face, He repays him.
And you shall keep the commandment and the statutes and the judgments that I am commanding you today to do them.
And it shall be, as a result of your listening to these judgments and keeping and doing them, the Lord your God will keep for you the covenant and the loving devotion that He swore to your fathers.

And He will love you and bless you and multiply you, and He will bless the fruit of your womb and the fruit of your ground—your grain and your new wine and your oil, the offspring of your herds and the lambs of your flocks—on the ground that He swore to your fathers to give you.
Blessed will you be above all peoples; there shall not be in you barren male or female or in your livestock.
And the Lord will remove from you every sickness, and all the evil diseases of Egypt that you knew He will not put on you, but He will lay them on all who hate you.
And you shall consume all the peoples that the Lord your God is giving to you. Your eye shall not pity them, and you shall not serve their gods, for that is a snare to you.
When you say in your heart: These nations are more numerous than I; how can I dispossess them?
You shall not be afraid of them; remember, you shall remember what the Lord your God did to Pharaoh and to all Egypt:

the great trials that your eyes saw, and the signs and the wonders, and the strong hand, and the outstretched arm by which the Lord your God brought you out. So the Lord your God will do to all the peoples whom you fear from before them.
And also the hornet the Lord your God will send among them, until those who remain and those who hide from your face have perished.
You shall not be terrified before them, for the Lord your God is in your midst, a great and awesome God.
And the Lord your God will dispossess these nations from before you little by little. You will not be able to finish them quickly, lest the beasts of the field multiply against you.
And the Lord your God will give them before you, and He will throw them into great confusion until they are destroyed.
And He will give their kings into your hand, and you will destroy their name from under the heavens. No man shall stand before you, until you have destroyed them.

The carved images of their gods you shall burn in the fire. You shall not covet the silver and gold that is on them, and you shall not take it for yourself, lest you be ensnared by it; for it is an abomination of the Lord your God.

And you shall not bring an abomination into your house, and you become devoted to destruction like it. Detesting, you shall detest it, and abhorring, you shall abhor it, for it is devoted to destruction.
